rules.obligation: Manufacturer must plan, establish, document, implement, maintain and update a post-market surveillance system proportionate to risk class and appropriate to device type.
rules.agent_instruction: Reflect that PMS findings derive from a documented system proportionate to device risk. Do not narrate the system's existence — present its outputs.

rules.obligation: Class I manufacturers prepare a Post-Market Surveillance Report (PMSR), updated when necessary and made available to the competent authority on request.
rules.agent_instruction: For Class I devices, state explicitly that the regulatory obligation is a PMSR (Article 85) and this PSUR-format document is prepared voluntarily for internal governance.

rules.obligation: Class IIa, IIb and III manufacturers prepare a PSUR summarising results and conclusions of PMS data analysis, including PMCF rationale and CAPA. Class IIb/III/implantable: annual cadence. Class IIa: at least every two years.
rules.agent_instruction: Confirm the device's PSUR cadence matches the EU MDR class. Do not cite the article number in narrative.

rules.obligation: PSUR must contain a clear statement on whether the benefit-risk profile has been adversely impacted, has improved, or remains unchanged.
rules.agent_instruction: Section A and Section M must each contain an explicit benefit-risk conclusion using the exact schema enum value (e.g. NOT_ADVERSELY_IMPACTED_UNCHANGED, ADVERSELY_IMPACTED). Do not hedge or use speculative language.

rules.obligation: PSUR must summarise the main findings of the PMCF and PMS data, including incident, complaint and trend analyses.
rules.agent_instruction: Section F owns complaint counts and rates. Section G owns trend analysis. Section M references both without restating the underlying numbers.

rules.obligation: PSUR must report sales volume and an estimate of the size and other characteristics of the population using the device, with frequency of use where practicable.
rules.agent_instruction: Section C must include the counting method (units distributed, units implanted, episodes of use), the same denominator used for rates in Section F, and a population-exposure estimate independent of sales when applicable.

rules.obligation: Serious incidents and field safety corrective actions must be reported to competent authorities; serious public-health threats within 2 days, deaths within 10 days, other serious incidents within 15 days.
rules.agent_instruction: Section D must list every serious incident with IMDRF Annex A device-problem term, Annex F harm term, region, and the actual complaint reference number. Do not invent reference numbers.

rules.obligation: PMS plan must define methods, indicators, threshold values for benefit-risk reassessment, and trend reporting methodology.
rules.agent_instruction: Reference the PMS plan when describing the trend methodology (UCL definition, Western Electric rules, action thresholds).

rules.obligation: Class I non-sterile, non-measuring devices are self-certified via Declaration of Conformity. There is no Notified Body involvement.
rules.agent_instruction: Do NOT reference Notified Body reviews, certificates, opinions, audit findings or actions. Do NOT state that the report will be 'submitted to' or 'reviewed by' a Notified Body. Cover-page NB fields are administrative only.
